title: aconitum nipponicum subsp. nipponicum
intro: Aconitum nipponicum subsp. nipponicum, also known as the Japanese monk's hood or Japanese aconitum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Aconitum genus. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese and Japanese medicine, where it has been used for centuries to treat pain, fever, and inflammation. The plant's roots and rhizomes are rich in alkaloids, such as aconitine, which have been found to have analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its health benefits in treating conditions like arthritis, gout, and skin infections. In herbal preparations, Aconitum nipponicum subsp. nipponicum is often used in decoctions, infusions, or tinctures, although its use requires caution due to its potential toxicity and requires guidance from a qualified practitioner.
